description: Canonical event vocabulary for Epic 5 music-event inference and review.

categories.name: transition
categories.description: Structural shifts that change energy direction or listener expectation.

categories.name: drop_variant
categories.description: Release-phase event types that refine the parent drop concept.

categories.name: sustained_state
categories.description: Persistent song states that describe plateau, loop, or held-tension behavior.

categories.name: vocal_phrase
categories.description: Vocal-led moments that often need phrase-aware lighting treatment.

categories.name: arrangement_change
categories.description: Layering changes that reshape density without necessarily creating a full transition.

categories.name: accent
categories.description: Short impact-style events that mark emphasis inside a larger section or phrase.

categories.name: rhythmic_texture
categories.description: Descriptive patterns of rhythmic movement, often validated by stem loudness or FFT transients.

events.name: build
events.description: Escalating pre-release energy, often marked by rising overall RMS loudness, increasing high-frequency FFT energy, and/or increasing onset density.

events.name: breakdown
events.description: A deliberate reduction in arrangement, density, or intensity, often marked by decreasing overall RMS loudness, spectral simplification, and/or reduced active stems.

events.name: drop
events.description: The main tension-release event where built energy resolves into peak motion or impact, characterized by a significant increase in overall RMS loudness, rhythmic activation, and spectral expansion.

events.name: drop_explode
events.description: A drop variant with explosive onset, large spectral expansion, and obvious impact emphasis.

events.name: drop_groove
events.description: A groove-led drop with sustained rhythmic drive rather than a single oversized hit.

events.name: drop_punch
events.description: A compact, punch-forward drop that emphasizes transient impact over wide release bloom.

events.name: soft_release
events.description: A release event that resolves tension with a smoother or less aggressive energy landing.

events.name: no_drop_plateau
events.description: A plateau after expectation-building where no full drop materializes, characterized by sustained mid-level RMS loudness and a stable FFT profile without significant transients.

events.name: fake_drop
events.description: A fake-out release cue that withholds the expected drop payoff, often indicated by a brief increase in high-frequency FFT energy without sustained bass activation.

events.name: tension_hold
events.description: A held state where tension persists without resolving into a drop or reset, often showing sustained high-frequency FFT energy and stable but elevated RMS loudness.

events.name: pause_break
events.description: A stop-time, pause, or near-silent interruption used as a structural punctuation point, characterized by near-zero RMS loudness across all stems and minimal FFT activity.

events.name: anthem_call
events.description: A strong lead-vocal call moment meant to frame a crowd-facing or singalong phrase, typically showing high vocal stem RMS loudness and clear vocal fundamental frequency.

events.name: call_response
events.description: A phrase pair where one motif is answered by another voice, line, or instrumental phrase, often indicated by alternating peaks in vocal stem RMS and instrumental stem RMS.

events.name: hook_phrase
events.description: A recurring hook or refrain phrase that acts as a memorable focal line, often indicated by repeated vocal melodic contour and consistent vocal stem RMS profile across occurrences.

events.name: vocal_spotlight
events.description: A voice-led window where the vocal envelope dominates, often triggered by the removal of rhythmic elements like the kick drum. Characterized by high vocal stem RMS relative to other stems and clear presence in 1kHz-4kHz FFT bands.

events.name: vocal_tail
events.description: A trailing vocal decay or handoff where the vocal energy falls away while the section continues underneath, marked by decreasing vocal stem RMS loudness and vocal stem activity falling below a threshold.

events.name: energy_reset
events.description: A clear reset of intensity or momentum before the next section re-establishes direction, often indicated by an abrupt drop in overall RMS loudness and spectral simplification.

events.name: layer_add
events.description: An arrangement moment where material is added and density or width meaningfully increases, typically showing an increase in overall RMS loudness, addition of new active stems, and/or expansion of FFT activity.

events.name: layer_remove
events.description: An arrangement moment where material is stripped back and density meaningfully decreases, typically showing a decrease in overall RMS loudness, reduction of active stems, and/or simplification of FFT activity.

events.name: impact_hit
events.description: A short impact marker with strong transient emphasis or downbeat punctuation, characterized by a sharp, short-duration peak in overall RMS loudness, broadband FFT spike, and high onset strength.

events.name: stinger
events.description: A compact one-shot accent or punctuation phrase that ends or punctuates a moment, often a short-duration, high-energy event with specific timbral characteristics.

events.name: groove_loop
events.description: A sustained rhythmic loop state with stable pulse and repetition-driven momentum, characterized by periodic drum stem RMS peaks and consistent low-frequency FFT activity.

events.name: atmospheric_plateau
events.description: A persistent spacious or ambient plateau with low-volatility energy and texture emphasis, typically showing low overall RMS loudness, diffuse FFT activity without sharp transients, and minimal drum stem activity.

events.name: percussion_break
events.description: A percussion-dominant pocket where drums carry the motion while harmonic and vocal material pull back, characterized by high drum stem RMS loudness, low harmonic/vocal stem RMS, and prominent mid-range FFT activity (snare/hats).

events.name: instrumental_bed
events.description: A support-driven instrumental passage where accompaniment or texture carries the section more than the lead vocal, typically showing low vocal stem RMS loudness and sustained harmonic/bass stem RMS loudness.

events.name: heartbeat_pattern
events.description: A syncopated, low-frequency pulse resembling a heartbeat, usually driven by the kick drum.

events.name: four_on_the_floor
events.description: A steady, driving rhythmic pulse on every beat, typically in 4/4 time.
